name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-43
title: scope-guard.py silently ALLOWS write when scope marker references unknown feature
description: hooks/scope-guard.py decide() at lines 152-186: when .rabbit-scope-active marker exists in ancestor chain, code reads marker content, calls find_feature_path(REPO_ROOT, scope_feature). If find_feature_path returns None (feature not in registry, lookup failed, find-feature.py missing), the code falls through to line 186 and returns True with 'ALLOW (under active scope)'. This silently bypasses scope enforcement: a marker file with invalid/typo feature name allows ANY write to ANY path. The default-deny invariant is violated by a single bad marker. The code should DENY with a clear error message ('unknown feature in scope marker') when feature lookup fails.

history.note: Fixed in Wave 2 PR. session-init R1 implementation restored (BUG-17); sync-check drift color now RED (BUG-34); tests use isolated temp-dir fixtures (BUG-40); scope-guard DENIES on unresolvable feature (BUG-43); @-import handling fixed (BUG-78). 9 new e2e tests; all 39 rabbit-cage suites pass.
